reason: Both answers correctly diagnose the severity and converge on nearly identical action items (detection telemetry, resilient selectors using ytd-* tags/ARIA labels, remote config for fast fixes, API migration check, channel diversification). The key differentiator is the "capture direct user relationship (accounts/email)" point: Answer B states this explicitly and separately ("Convert extension users into direct users fast: get accounts/emails early so a broken extension doesn't mean losing the relationship"), while Answer A only implies it obliquely via "the web app itself" as one diversification option, without framing account/email capture as a distinct mitigation. B also adds accurate, non-padding technical detail (synthetic monitoring, MutationObservers for YouTube's SPA behavior) and organizes actions on a clearer week/month/quarter timeline. A includes a nice "break-glass plan" idea B lacks, but this is a smaller addition than B's more complete coverage of the direct-relationship capture point, which is one of the four core key points.
